title: ulmus × dampieri
intro: Ulmus × dampieri, commonly known as the hybrid elm tree, is a cross between the English elm (Ulmus procera) and the Camperdown elm (Ulmus glabra 'Camperdownii'). The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ulmus × dampieri include reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and flu.
